question.text: The option writer (seller) of a put option has:

answer.text: An obligation to buy from the put buyer if the option is exercised
The right to buy at the strike price
An obligation to sell at the strike price
No obligation as premium has been received

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The put seller receives premium and in return has the OBLIGATION to buy from the put buyer at the strike price if exercised. The premium compensates for this risk. **The Trap:** The put buyer has the right to sell. The call seller has the obligation to sell. Receiving premium creates obligation, not immunity. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers put writer obligations in equity options. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Put sellers must be prepared to buy the commodity at the strike price — this is only suitable if you believe the price won't fall below your breakeven (strike minus premium).
